scope: Phase B — Eval Runner Upgrade + Blind Independent Judge Run
actions_taken: Upgraded eval runner from stub to scoring engine (scripts/evals/run_skill_eval.py)
Implemented heuristic keyword scorer (FOUND UNFIT — false negatives on negation contexts)
Implemented blind independent LLM judge via delegate_task
Saved dogfood-run-1 responses to evals/skill/runs/dogfood-run-1/ (baseline/ + skill-equipped/)
Ran blind judge: anonymized responses as A/B, judge scored without knowing agent identity
Generated score-report.json with dual scoring methods documented

blind_judge.judge_note: E6 baseline scored 0 — potentially too harsh. Both A3/A4 classifications are defensible per rubric. Judge consistency issue flagged.

heuristic_keyword.verdict: UNFIT — produces false negatives on negation contexts. Demonstrates need for LLM judge.

key_findings: Blind independent judge confirms skill-equipped > baseline on 5/8 cases
Skill impact areas: explicit governance framing (E3), execution frame structure (E5), systematic classification (E6), permission model application (E7), source-first discipline (E4)
Judge is same model (deepseek-v4-pro) — not truly independent
Heuristic keyword scoring is broken for governance eval — LLM judge required
E6 scoring inconsistency shows need for judge calibration across runs

deliverables.runner: scripts/evals/run_skill_eval.py — Phase B: scoring + report generation

remaining_debt: DEP-JUDGE-INDEPENDENT: Judge is same model as agents. Need different model for true independence.
DEP-JUDGE-CALIBRATION: E6 baseline scored 0 — classification disagreement may indicate rubric ambiguity or judge bias.
DEP-RUNNER-AUTO: Runner still requires manual response collection. No end-to-end agent→judge pipeline.
DEP-MULTI-MODEL: Only tested on deepseek-v4-pro. No weaker model baseline.
degraded_reasons: Independent judge is same model family — not truly independent
next_phase: Phase C — Advisory Checkers (skill-safety, receipt-integrity, mcp-permissions). Advisory only, not blocking.
